report.summary: Built 2 new learn guides: Rollup-as-a-Service (RaaS) Providers Compared 2026 covering Conduit, Caldera, Gelato, and AltLayer with tech stack breakdowns, comparison tables, and decision frameworks; and Staking-as-a-Service Providers Compared 2026 covering Lido, Kiln, Figment, P2P.org, and Coinbase with market share data, fee analysis, institutional vs retail guidance, and LST ecosystem overview.

sections.title: Research Brief
items: RaaS market valued at $75.4M in 2024, projected to $354M by 2032 (20.5% CAGR). Gelato and Conduit lead market share. Over 100 rollup chains now live in 2026.

Key RaaS providers: Conduit (no-code OP Stack + Orbit deployment, Zora/Aevo/Degen chains), Caldera (40+ infra integrations, Manta Pacific), Gelato (full-stack managed ops, auto-scaling RPC), AltLayer (restaked rollups via AVS with MACH/SQUAD/VITAL)
Modular stack has matured: execution (OP Stack, Orbit, ZK Stack, Polygon CDK), settlement (Ethereum), DA (blobs, Celestia, EigenDA, Avail), bridging (Hyperlane, LayerZero)
Institutional staking market hit $5.8B AUM in 2024, projected to $33.3B by 2033. 10 major entities control 60%+ of Ethereum staking.
Lido dominates at 24.2% (8.7M ETH, 39 node operators). Coinbase at 5.1% (1.8M ETH). Figment fastest-growing at 4.5% (+344K ETH recently). Kiln manages ~$4B AUM across 10+ protocols.
P2P.org supports 50+ PoS blockchains, developer-friendly via DataHub API. Non-custodial model with no minimums on some protocols.

sections.title: Content Created
items: Rollup-as-a-Service (RaaS) Guide 2026 — 501-line learn guide covering RaaS definition, why it matters in 2026, the 4-layer modular tech stack, deep dives on 4 major providers (Conduit, Caldera, Gelato, AltLayer) with comparison table, decision framework for choosing a provider, risks and limitations (vendor lock-in, centralized sequencing, liquidity fragmentation), and 6 FAQs. Includes 5 internal links to related guides.

Staking-as-a-Service Providers Compared 2026 — 728-line learn guide covering STaaS definition and market context, staking landscape data (market shares, validator economics), deep dives on 5 major providers (Lido, Kiln, Figment, P2P.org, Coinbase) with fee structures and custody models, institutional vs retail decision matrix, LST ecosystem (stETH, cbETH, rETH) and yield stacking strategies, risk analysis (slashing, centralization, depegging), and 6 FAQs. Includes 5 internal links to related guides.

sections.title: SEO Optimization
items: RaaS guide: title 'Rollup-as-a-Service (RaaS) Guide 2026 — Conduit, Caldera, Gelato & AltLayer Compared | degen0x', target keywords: raas, rollup as a service, conduit raas, caldera blockchain, gelato raas, altlayer restaked rollups

Staking guide: title 'Staking-as-a-Service Providers Compared 2026 — Lido, Kiln, Figment, P2P & More | degen0x', target keywords: staking as a service, staking providers compared, kiln staking, figment staking, institutional staking
Both pages include Article + FAQ structured data schemas (JSON-LD) via combineSchemas for rich snippet eligibility
Both pages include OpenGraph article metadata with publishedTime, Twitter card metadata, keyword arrays
Both pages added to PUBLISH_QUEUE in published-pages.ts
Fixed JSX escape issues (unescaped > characters) in staking guide — corrected to {'>'}22% and {'>'}15% syntax
